rag_rules: AGREE diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers or invalid helpers in guarantees should be fixed by tracing the first unresolved reference or deleting the invalid clause
Implementation-level bindings or references in component type annexes should be moved to implementation annexes or removed
Avoid generating AGREE clauses that reference opaque Boolean data or perform range comparisons on non-numeric types
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings
Generated AGREE code must only reference visible identifiers and types from the current model and its dependencies
Validator diagnostics should guide minimal repairs that preserve unrelated architecture elements
When a guarantee references a port, ensure the reference is valid in the current scope (type vs implementation)
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations in the same annex for AGREE expressions
Component type contracts may not reference implementation-specific subcomponent paths or outputs
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers unless explicitly allowed by configured dependencies
anti_patterns: Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics unless the requirement explicitly demands it
Do not generate range comparisons or 0/1 equality checks on Boolean or opaque data ports
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
Do not reference unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations in AGREE guarantees
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about Byte_Count property associations
Component type annexes for implementation-level bindings
